data.title: POET Technologies to voluntarily delist from TSX, maintains Nasdaq listing
data.description:
• POET Technologies (NASDAQ:POET) will proceed with the voluntary delisting of its common shares from the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • The delisting is expected to take effect at the close of trading on or about August 27, 2025.
  • POET’s shares will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “POET.”
  • The company has received approval for the delisting and completed the necessary application process.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: QYOU Media launches up to $750,000 private placement of units
data.description:
QYOU Media (OTCQB:QYOUF) announced on Monday an up to $750,000 non-brokered private placement offering of up to 25M units priced at $0.03 per unit.

Each unit will be comprised of one share and three-quarters of one share purchase warrant.

Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one share at $0.06 per share for a period of 24 months from the close of the offering.

Net proceeds will be used for the repayment of outstanding loans, payment of amounts due from the acquisition of Chatterbox Technologies, and working capital and general corporate purposes.

The offering is expected to close on or about September 5.

data.title: La Rosa Holdings regains compliance with Nasdaq continued listing requirements
data.description:
La Rosa Holdings (NASDAQ:LRHC) has received formal notification from Nasdaq that it has regained compliance with the Nasdaq Listing Rule.

La Rosa Holdings (NASDAQ:LRHC) is down nearly 8% premarket.

data.title: Bank7 renews 2-year stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Bank7 (NASDAQ:BSVN) has renewed its stock repurchase program for another two years.
  • The program authorizes the repurchase of up to 750,000 shares of common stock.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Alaska Air gains after Raymond James turns bullish on the second-half outlook
data.description:
Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK) traded higher in early action on Monday after Raymond James upgraded the airline stock to an Outperform rating after having it set at Market Perform.

Analyst Savanthi Syth said current demand and competitive capacity trends increase the conviction in the firm's earnings forecasts on Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK), as well as the attractiveness of the risk-reward profile. 

"While remaining concerned about the increased complexity of the business model, particularly the widebody aircraft exposure, we have gained a greater appreciation of Alaska’s measured approach to taking on this risk," updated Syth. 

Syth thinks the outcome at Spirit Airlines (OTC:SAVEQ) will be an important development for the U.S. airline industry, noting that Alaska (ALK) is already a beneficiary of the struggles at Spirit. "While competitive trends are largely benign across most U.S. airlines, it is particularly attractive at Alaska with competitive seats in its markets scheduled to be (2)/(3)% y/y in 3Q/4Q25 vs. 0/2% in 1Q/2Q25," highlighted Syth.

Shares of Alaska Air Group (ALK) moved 1.2% higher premarket on Monday to $60.20 after gaining 8.3% on Friday. The 52-week high for the airline stock is $78.08.

data.title: FEI-Zyfer announces contract award for $3.14 million
data.description:
FEI-Zyfer, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Frequency Electronics (NASDAQ:FEIM) announced the receipt of a contract from the Defense Logistics Agency, Aviation Division, at Redstone Arsenal, AL, for additional systems supporting the Patriot Missile system.

The base year (Year 1) contract value is $3.14 million dollars, with a period of performance through October 30, 2026.

In addition, the contract includes delivery options for Year 2 of $1.71 million, and a Year 3 option of $1.80 million dollars.

data.title: Stock index futures tick down after Dow's record close; focus on Nvidia results
data.description:
Stock index futures were slightly lower on Monday, after the Dow notched a record close on Friday following Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s remarks at Jackson Hole.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.4%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.29%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.72%.

The Chicago Fed National Activity Index ticked down in July as production slipped into the red.

Powell's speech on Friday led to a rally in stocks, while odds of a Fed rate cut in September also rose. 

"Powell’s speech at Jackson Hole last week was classic Powell. That is not a terribly good thing. Powell signaled an increased probability of a September rate cut to counter the damage of trade taxes on the U.S. economy," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Markets liked that. But this was essentially the “data dependent” mantra padded with additional rhetoric, Donovan added.

"Markets ended last week in a buoyant mood as a dovish tilt by Powell at Jackson Hole left investors increasingly confident on upcoming Fed easing. While Fed news will continue to draw attention this week, the focus will also shift to a slew of inflation releases," Deutsche Bank's     Peter Sidorov said.

Investors will also keep their eye on the Nvidia (NVDA) results slated to come on Wednesday after the market close. 

"Finally, the big event in corporate earnings will be Nvidia's results, which come as tech stocks had seen their biggest five-day pullback since April prior to Friday’s rally," Sidorov added.

The July PCE price index will come in on Friday.

data.title: POET Technologies set to delist from TSX Venture Exchange on Wednesday market close
data.description:
POET Technologies (NASDAQ:POET) announced on Monday a voluntary delisting from the TSX Venture Exchange, expected to be effective as of Wednesday, August 27, market close.

The delisting from the TSXV will not affect the company's listing on Nasdaq.

data.title: Chipotle launches a value-focused family meal offering
data.description:
Chipotle Mexican Grill (NYSE:CMG) announced on Monday a new "Build-Your-Own Chipotle" family meal that will be available for digital order starting on August 26. The company sees the offering as an accessible, value-focused alternative for families, gatherings, and back-to-school routines, emphasizing customization and ease. 

The fast-casual restaurant operator said the family meal is designed to serve four to six people. The digital-exclusive menu option was highlighted as allowing customers to create a customized Chipotle experience at home with family-style portions of main ingredients, offering a choice of protein (including chicken, steak, barbacoa, carnitas, or Sofritas), rice, beans, a premium side such as guacamole or Queso Blanco, three salsas or toppings, lettuce, cheese, two large bags of chips, eight tortillas, and utensils for six. 

The meal is being positioned by Chipotle (NYSE:CMG) as a convenient solution for group occasions requiring no advance planning and can be ready for pickup in as little as 15 minutes. Chipotle (CMG) is promoting the launch with a limited-time $10 discount on the first "Build-Your-Own Chipotle" order through its app or website.

Chipotle (CMG) operates over 3,800 restaurants globally, maintaining a focus on responsibly sourced ingredients and a digitally driven guest experience. 

Shares of Chipotle (CMG) fell 0.2% in premarket trading to $43.55 vs. the 52-week range of $41.18 to $66.74.

data.title: The Home Depot extends tender offer for GMS
data.description:
• The Home Depot (NYSE:HD) said on Monday that its wholly owned subsidiary, Gold Acquisition Sub has extended its tender to acquire all outstanding shares of GMS (GMS) for $110 per share in cash.
  • The offer, initially set to expire on August 22, will now remain open until 11:59 p.m. ET on September 3, 2025.
  • As of the original deadline, about 29.3 million GMS shares or 77% of the total had been tendered.
  • The deal, announced on June 29, 2025, still needs approval from Canadian regulators and requires a majority of shares to be tendered before it can close.
  • Source: Press Release
